title: Deliver campaign-decomposition verdict (Stage 1 backlog)
description: Phase-2 requirement-driven chain (archive/2026-07-09-phase2-requirement-chain-design.md §3.2). Output shape for the Deliver Agent's STAGE-1 campaign decomposition in the campaign_bootstrap pre-chain: the SIGNED research brief decomposed into an ordered MILESTONE backlog (not sub-sprints — each milestone's sub-sprints are Stage 2, reusing deliver-plan-verdict.schema.json). Parsed ONLY in loop_mode: campaign_bootstrap after the Gate-1 sign-off; no existing mode touches it. Per-milestone modules/layers feed the deterministic envelope guard against the Gate-1 signed_envelope SNAPSHOT; they are recorded in the decompose sidecar, never in the emitted campaign plan (campaign-plan.schema.json is closed). maxItems 12 is a deterministic bound on Stage-2 spawn fan-out (design §3.2 [R0.2 B-3]).

goal.description: The user-facing campaign goal, distilled from the SIGNED brief. Projected verbatim into campaign-plan.json `goal`.

milestones.description: Ordered milestone backlog. Order is the default execution order (depends_on may add constraints; the projection validates the DAG via campaign.topological_order).

id.description: Path-safe milestone id (mirrors campaign-plan.schema.json milestone id constraints).

acceptance_bar.description: One-line close condition for the milestone (projected into the plan; the milestone-close Acceptance gate judges against the signed intent contract + this bar).

modules.description: Modules this milestone will touch. Union (with Stage-2 sub-sprint modules) is checked against the Gate-1 signed_envelope snapshot's modules_in_scope by the envelope guard. Sidecar-only — not projected into the plan.

items.description: Δ-9 fix-layer (same closed enum as deliver-plan-verdict.schema.json).

layers.description: Δ-9 fix-layers this milestone will touch. Same envelope-guard role as modules. Sidecar-only.

covers_req_ids.description: Δ-19 §3.4 requirement ids this milestone covers. Coverage claims REQUIRE a wired requirement ledger to verify (design §3.3(c) [R0 B-4]): with no ledger, ANY covers_req_ids is a deterministic refusal; with a ledger, every rid must exist in it. Cross-milestone uniqueness enforced at projection (campaign.py Campaign ingress semantics).

depends_on.description: Milestone ids that must complete before this one (validated as a DAG by topological_order at projection).

functional_acceptance.description: Optional per-milestone acceptance class. When a wired ledger classifies a covered rid as user_facing, the projection FORCES browser_e2e (OW-AUTO / PR#7 semantics) and OW-M3 re-verifies.

milestone_signals.description: Track 1 / universal-skill-mounting: optional SIGNED task-affinity signals for the milestone (closed vocabulary; projected into the plan's milestone_signals and drift-guarded by milestone_signals_digest at sign time).
